title: Carer's Allowance
jurisdiction: England and Wales (in Scotland this is Carer Support Payment)

effective.uprating: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order 2026 (S.I. 2026/148); earnings limit pegged to 16 hours at the National Living Wage (16 × £12.75 = £204)

plainly: £86.45 a week for caring 35 hours or more for someone on a disability benefit. Over State Pension age the two usually overlap and nothing is paid — but claim anyway: the entitlement itself adds £48.15 a week to Pension Credit.

questions.label: Your date of birth
questions.help: Only used against the age condition — it never leaves this page.
questions.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.70: aged 16 or over

questions.label: Do you live in Scotland?
questions.rule: Carer's assistance is devolved: Carer Support Payment, from Social Security Scotland

questions.label: Do you spend 35 hours or more a week caring for someone?
questions.help: Add it all up — helping with washing, meals, medication, appointments, keeping them safe. It does not need to be one block, or under one roof.
questions.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.70(1)(a) (regularly and substantially engaged in caring); Invalid Care Allowance Regulations 1976 (S.I. 1976/409) reg 4 (35 hours)

questions.label: Does the person you care for get Attendance Allowance, PIP or ADP daily living, DLA care (middle or higher), Pension Age Disability Payment, or Child Disability Payment (middle or higher)?
questions.help: If they have not claimed theirs yet, that claim comes first — yours chains from it (the Attendance Allowance answer above may help them).
questions.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.70(2) (caring for a severely disabled person, defined by receipt of a qualifying benefit)

questions.label: Your own weekly earnings, after tax, National Insurance and allowable expenses (not your partner's)
questions.help: Enter 0 if none. Some costs are deducted before the test — half of pension contributions, some care costs while you work.
questions.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.70(1)(b); Invalid Care Allowance Regulations 1976 reg 8 (the earnings limit: £204 for 2026–27)

questions.label: Your own weekly State Pension (not your partner's)
questions.help: The same figure as your State Pension above if you are single; 0 if you do not get one yet.
questions.rule: Social Security (Overlapping Benefits) Regulations 1979 (S.I. 1979/597): Carer's Allowance and State Pension overlap — one is set against the other

values.plainly: The Carer's Allowance rate (2026–27: £86.45 a week).
values.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.70(5), Sch 4 Part III, as uprated by S.I. 2026/148

values.plainly: What would actually be paid: the rate, less your own State Pension (they overlap — you cannot be paid both in full).

gates.because: In Scotland this is Carer Support Payment, claimed from Social Security Scotland — close cousin rules, and moving between the two schemes has a 13-week grace period.
gates.rule: Devolved: Social Security Scotland, Carer Support Payment
Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.70; gov.uk Carer's Allowance eligibility

verdicts.because: The amount shown is what would be paid after your State Pension is set against the £86.45 rate — the two overlap. It counts as income for Pension Credit, and it brings the carer addition with it. One check first: payment of Carer's Allowance can end a severe-disability amount in the benefits of the person you care for — where they get one, take advice before claiming.
verdicts.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.70; S.I. 1979/597

verdicts.because: Entitled — but not paid: your State Pension is at least the £86.45 rate, and the two overlap. Claim anyway. An underlying entitlement is exactly what the Pension Credit carer question above means: it adds the £48.15 carer addition and raises the earnings disregard to £20 — in gov.uk's own words, people with higher pensions get increased Pension Credit instead. And unpaid entitlement does not touch the cared-for person's own benefits.
verdicts.rule: S.I. 1979/597 (overlap); State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.2(8) (the addition an entitlement earns)

verdicts.because: Your earnings are over the £204 weekly limit — and it is a cliff edge: a penny over loses the whole allowance. Before giving up, check what counts: the test is after tax, National Insurance, half of any pension contributions, and some care costs while you work.
verdicts.rule: Invalid Care Allowance Regulations 1976 reg 8 (the earnings limit, 2026–27: £204)

verdicts.because: Carer's Allowance turns on two facts: 35 or more hours of care a week, for someone who gets a qualifying disability benefit. If the person you care for has not claimed theirs yet, start there — the Attendance Allowance answer above may be the first link in the chain.
verdicts.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.70(1)(a), (2)

not_asked: the name of the person you care for — only whether they get a qualifying benefit
not_encoded: the interaction warning, in full: while Carer's Allowance is IN PAYMENT for someone's care, that person's severe-disability amounts stop (Pension Credit and Housing Benefit both encode that rule above) — where they have one, the household can be worse off; take advice first. An unpaid underlying entitlement causes no such loss
full-time education (21 or more hours of supervised study a week disqualifies — not asked because it is rare over State Pension age; if it applies to you, it applies)
breaks in care (hospital stays, respite — up to 4 weeks in 26 can be ignored, 12 in hospital)
backdating (up to 3 months) and Carer's Credit (the National Insurance protection for under-35-hour or over-limit carers)
only one person can be paid for one cared-for person, and one carer cannot double the award by caring for two

passports.name: Pension Credit carer addition (£48.15 a week)
passports.note: earned by the entitlement itself, paid or not — answer yes to the carer question above and watch it appear.

passports.name: Housing Benefit carer premium
passports.note: the same £48.15 shape, in the rent calculation above.

sources_checked.what: Rate £86.45 and earnings limit £204 (2026–27) — internally consistent twice over: £83.30 × the 3.8% uprating, and 16 hours × £12.75 National Living Wage
Qualifying benefits list; 35 hours; aged 16+; full-time education exclusion
The overlap and its consolation: State Pension at or above the rate means nothing is paid, and 'increased Pension Credit instead'
Scotland: Carer Support Payment, 13-week transition on moving
